chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

questions.question: What political policy of the British angered many Indian rulers before 1857?
questions.answer: The Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Why did the annexation of Awadh create resentment among people?
questions.answer: Because it displaced many soldiers, officials and landlords.

questions.question: Why were Indian sepoys dissatisfied with their position in the British army?
questions.answer: They faced discrimination in pay, promotions and treatment.

questions.question: Why did many Indians believe the British threatened their religion?
questions.answer: They feared British reforms would force them to change religious practices.

questions.question: Why did the rebels choose the Mughal emperor as their leader?
questions.answer: He symbolized legitimate authority in India.

questions.question: Why was Delhi strategically important during the revolt?
questions.answer: It was the political and symbolic centre of Mughal power.

questions.question: What economic hardship contributed to the Revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: Heavy taxes and destruction of traditional industries.

questions.question: Why did peasants participate in the Revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: They were burdened by heavy revenue demands.

questions.question: How did the revolt spread from Meerut?
questions.answer: Rebel sepoys marched to Delhi and encouraged others to join.

questions.question: Why was the revolt mainly concentrated in northern and central India?
questions.answer: These regions had a high number of sepoys and discontented rulers.

questions.question: Why did the rebels fail to achieve unity?
questions.answer: Different groups had different aims and lacked coordination.

questions.question: Why did the British eventually suppress the revolt?
questions.answer: They had superior weapons and better military organization.

questions.question: Why did the British punish rebel leaders harshly after the revolt?
questions.answer: To prevent further rebellions.

questions.question: What administrative change occurred after the revolt of 1857?
questions.answer: The British Crown took direct control of India.

questions.question: Why did the British reorganize the army after 1857?
questions.answer: To reduce the risk of another rebellion.

questions.question: Why did the British increase the number of European soldiers after the revolt?
questions.answer: To maintain stronger control over the army.

questions.question: Why did the British seek support from Indian princes after the revolt?
questions.answer: To ensure political stability and loyalty.

questions.question: Why were British policies towards religion changed after the revolt?
questions.answer: The British feared religious interference might cause unrest.

questions.question: Why is the Revolt of 1857 called a turning point in Indian history?
questions.answer: It led to major administrative and political changes.

questions.question: Why did the revolt inspire later nationalist movements?
questions.answer: It showed that Indians could challenge colonial rule.

questions.question: Why do historians describe the revolt as a widespread uprising?
questions.answer: Many social groups participated in the rebellion.

questions.question: Why is the Revolt of 1857 sometimes debated among historians?
questions.answer: Different historians interpret its causes and nature differently.

questions.question: Why did the British strengthen administrative control after the revolt?
questions.answer: To prevent future revolts.

questions.question: Why did the British try to avoid policies that angered Indian rulers after 1857?
questions.answer: They wanted to maintain loyalty among Indian princes.

questions.question: Why is studying the Revolt of 1857 important?
questions.answer: It helps understand early resistance to colonial rule.
